summary: Ukrainian forces are conducting long-range drone strikes, destroying transport vehicles 40+ km from the front and targeting equipment within a 100 km radius using a mix of cheap and expensive foreign loitering munitions. Belarusian forces have deployed armored vehicles with anti-drone protection, including the MZKT-233036-011 "Lis" and MZKT-490100-217. Russian anti-drone systems like "Elka" and "Yolka" have successfully intercepted Ukrainian strike drones through kinetic impact, with "Yolka" achieving a 1,300-meter intercept. Ukrainian forces are reducing FPV drone control frequency while increasing antenna size on the front lines, and the "Rubicon" Center reported 116 targets destroyed on April 6, 2026, including fixed-wing UAVs and personnel.
